resources.string: Camera permission is required to track movement.
✗ Phone is moving — prop it up or use a tripod
✗ Step back until the whole player is visible
△ Auto scale only (rough, ±15%) — Calibrate for accuracy
✗ No scale yet — stand in view or tap Calibrate
Tip: film from the side — speed across the screen measures true, speed toward the camera doesn\'t.
New here? Tap Help to see what every button does, in plain words.

Point the camera at the action. "Player" is how fast the person is moving and "Ball" is how fast the ball is moving, in km/h. "Peak ball" remembers the fastest ball speed so far — tap Reset peak to set it back to 0.

To turn what the camera sees into real km/h, the app needs to know how big things really are. Easiest: just stand fully in view (auto, rough). Better: tap Calibrate and mark two points that are a known distance apart (like two cones), or enter your height. Best: AR scale, if your phone supports it. The little line at the top tells you how good the current setup is — green is trustworthy, orange is rough, red means not ready.

Tap Start session before you practise. The app records everything: your speeds, distance, every kick, bowl and jump — plus a replay video. Tap Stop session when you\'re done and it\'s saved on your phone. Nothing is uploaded anywhere.

Your history. Tap any session to see its stats, a speed chart, tips, and the replay video. Hold a session to delete it. Export saves all your data as a file. Compare plays two replay videos on one screen so you can compare your technique.

A drill is simple practice with a score. Pick how many attempts (kicks or bowls) and a target speed. Every attempt is counted automatically — you hear a happy beep when you hit the target speed. At the end you get a scorecard: how many hit the target, your best and your average.

When a drill finishes, tap "Challenge a friend" to send your score as a short code (WhatsApp, SMS, anything). Your friend pastes that code into the Drill box on their phone and plays the exact same drill. When they finish, the app tells them who won.

For aiming practice. Tap Target, then tap the two opposite corners of your goal on the screen. A grid with 9 squares appears. From then on every shot records where it landed (or that it missed), and the session page shows a map of your shots — darker squares mean more shots landed there.

Very fast balls blur for the normal camera. For an accurate reading, record the kick or bowl as a slow-motion video with your phone\'s own camera app (120 or 240 fps), then open it under Slow-mo. The result is saved as a session too.
Voice on means the app speaks each ball speed out loud — handy when the phone is on a tripod and you\'re far away.
On a session\'s page, Share card turns your best numbers into a nice picture you can post or send to friends.
A live checklist for accurate speeds: phone held still, player fully in the picture, calibration ready. When everything is green you\'re good to go. One more tip: film from the side, not from behind.
Phone is moving — speeds unreliable. Use a tripod or prop it still.
Set your height for auto calibration
Height saved — auto calibration now assumes %1$d cm.
Please enter a height between 100 and 230 cm.
Calibration: none — waiting for a player…
Calibration: auto ±15%% (assumes %1$.2f m height) — Calibrate to improve
Tap two points on screen that are a known real-world distance apart (e.g. two cones).
Real distance between the two points
Please enter a valid distance in metres.
No sessions yet.\nStart one from the camera screen.
Tap a session for details · hold one to delete it
Top speed up %1$.0f%% vs last month
Top speed down %1$.0f%% vs last month
How fast you moved during the session. The dots along the bottom are ball moments — kicks and bowls.
Tap a moment below to jump the video right there.
Tips worked out from your own numbers in this session — not generic advice.

Practice with a score: you pick how many attempts and a target speed. Every kick or bowl counts as one attempt — beat the target speed and it\'s a hit. Got a challenge code from a friend? Paste it below and you\'ll play their exact drill instead.
%1$d of %2$d attempts hit %3$.0f km/h.\nBest %4$.1f km/h · average %5$.1f km/h.

Record a slow-motion video (120 or 240 fps) of a kick, bowl or throw, then pick it here. Analysing at the true capture rate measures fast balls far more accurately than the live camera.\n\nTip: film side-on, keep the whole body in frame.
Couldn\'t analyse that clip — nothing was tracked. Make sure the player and ball are visible.
Clip covers %1$.2f s of real time
Saved to Sessions with full stats and suggestions.
Point the crosshair at the ground where the player will be. When the distance locks, tap Use.
ARCore is not available on this device.
Camera unavailable for AR right now.
Measured on device with Movement Tracker
Paste a friend\'s challenge code here (optional)
That challenge code isn\'t valid — paste the whole message.
Challenge accepted — %1$d attempts at %2$.0f km/h. Beat %3$d hits!

I hit %1$d of %2$d attempts at %3$.0f km/h (best %4$.1f km/h) in Movement Tracker. Beat that!

Paste this code into Drill to accept:
%5$s
You beat your friend — they hit %1$d of %2$d (best %3$.1f km/h). 🏆
Your friend wins — they hit %1$d of %2$d (best %3$.1f km/h). Rematch?
Dead heat — %1$d hits each and a matching %2$.1f km/h best.
Drill %1$d/%2$d · best %3$.0f km/h · you %4$d hits · friend %5$d
Tap the two opposite corners of the goal (or any target area).
Target set — shot placement will be recorded.
Target cleared — the view changed size. Set it again.
Where your shots landed on the target. Darker squares = more shots there; misses count in the percentage below.
Cue a moment under each clip, then play both together.
Comparing needs two sessions with replay videos.
